facts.text: Farmingdale’s name first appeared when the Long Island Rail Road opened a station there in October 1841, naming the community after the line’s new stop.
The village grew around that 1841 LIRR station, a pattern echoed in many Long Island towns but unique to Farmingdale’s early 19th‑century boom.
In 1841 the LIRR’s Main Line station became a hub, later serving Ronkonkoma Branch trains and anchoring downtown’s commercial core.
The local library, founded by Toniann Contarino, digitizes historic photographs and hosts the Farmingdale‑Bethpage Historical Society’s archives.
Farmingdale houses the American Airpower Museum, a notable landmark that showcases historic aircraft and aviation history.
The Bethpage Purchase of 1695, when Thomas Powell bought 15 square miles from Native tribes, included the land that would become Farmingdale.
